 {"id":"bd-00u3","title":"Deprecate bd mol run after gt absorbs its semantics","description":"bd mol run is a convenience combo (pour + update + pin) that feels like orchestration but lives in the data layer. This blurs the bd/gt architectural boundary.

## Current State
- bd mol run = bd pour + bd update --status=in_progress + bd pin --for me
- gt spawn depends on bd mol run (shells out to it)

## Proposed Change
Once gt spawn inlines the pour+assign+pin logic:
1. Deprecate bd mol run with a warning pointing to gt spawn
2. Eventually remove bd mol run entirely

## Why
- bd should be pure data operations (pour, update, pin, close)
- gt should own orchestration (spawn, sling, hook, handoff)
- Standalone bd users can run the three commands separately or script them

## Blocked By
gastown issue gt-r7jj2 (Move mol run semantics from bd into gt spawn)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T00:59:03.667211-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:04:51.755145-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T01:04:51.755145-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-0134cc5a","title":"Fix auto-import creating duplicates instead of updating issues","description":"ROOT CAUSE: server_export_import_auto.go line 221 uses ResolveCollisions: true for ALL auto-imports. This is wrong.

Problem:
- ResolveCollisions is for branch merges (different issues with same ID)
- Auto-import should UPDATE existing issues, not create duplicates
- Every git pull creates NEW duplicate issues with different IDs
- Two agents ping-pong creating endless duplicates

Evidence:
- 31 duplicate groups found (bd duplicates)
- bd-236-246 are duplicates of bd-224-235
- Both agents keep pulling and creating more duplicates
- JSONL file grows endlessly with duplicates

The Fix:
Change checkAndAutoImportIfStale in server_export_import_auto.go:
- Remove ResolveCollisions: true (line 221)
- Use normal import logic that updates existing issues by ID
- Only use ResolveCollisions for explicit bd import --resolve-collisions

Impact: Critical - makes beads unusable for multi-agent workflows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-10-27T21:48:57.733846-07:00","updated_at":"2025-10-30T17:12:58.21084-07:00","closed_at":"2025-10-27T22:26:40.627239-07:00"}

 {"id":"bd-m62x","title":"Benchmark Suite for Critical Operations","description":"Extend existing benchmark suite with comprehensive benchmarks for critical operations at 10K-20K scale.

Existing benchmarks (keep these):
- cycle_bench_test.go - Cycle detection up to 5K issues (linear, tree, dense graphs)
- compact_bench_test.go - Compaction candidate queries (100 issues)
- internal/rpc/bench_test.go - Daemon vs direct mode comparison

New benchmarks to add in sqlite_bench_test.go (~10-12 total):
1. GetReadyWork - Simple, deep hierarchies, cross-linked (CRITICAL - not currently benchmarked)
2. SearchIssues - No filters, complex filters (CRITICAL - not currently benchmarked)
3. CreateIssue - Single issue creation
4. UpdateIssue - Status/priority/assignee changes
5. AddDependency - Extend to 10K/20K scale (currently only up to 5K)
6. JSONL Export - Full export performance
7. JSONL Import - Import performance

Scale levels:
- Large: 10K issues (5K open, 5K closed)
- XLarge: 20K issues (10K open, 10K closed)

Implementation:
- NEW FILE: internal/storage/sqlite/sqlite_bench_test.go
- Keep existing cycle_bench_test.go and compact_bench_test.go unchanged
- Build tag: //go:build bench
- Standard testing.B benchmarks
- b.ReportAllocs() for memory tracking
- Test both SQLite and JSONL-imported databases

Always generates CPU and memory profiles for analysis.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:22:43.770787-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-13T23:15:33.781023-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-13T23:15:33.781023-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m62x","depends_on_id":"bd-q13h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:24:02.668091-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-m62x","depends_on_id":"bd-zj8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-13T22:24:06.30131-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-m6r3","title":"Refactor: Extract shared getEpicChildren helper for swarm commands","description":"## Context
`getSwarmStatus` and `analyzeEpicForSwarm` in cmd/bd/swarm.go have nearly identical code for:
- Getting epic dependents via GetDependents
- Filtering to parent-child relationships
- Building childIDSet map
- Building dependency maps

## Implementation
1. Extract `getEpicChildren(ctx, store, epicID)` that returns:
   - `[]*types.Issue` - child issues
   - `map[string]bool` - child ID set
   - `map[string][]string` - dependency map (within children)
   - `error`

2. Refactor both `analyzeEpicForSwarm` and `getSwarmStatus` to use it

3. Consider defining a named interface for the store methods:
   ```go
   type SwarmStore interface {
       GetIssue(context.Context, string) (*types.Issue, error)
       GetDependents(context.Context, string) ([]*types.Issue, error)
       GetDependencyRecords(context.Context, string) ([]*types.Dependency, error)
   }
   ```

## Files
- cmd/bd/swarm.go (lines 150-248, 616-682)

## Acceptance
- No duplicated code for fetching epic children
- Both commands produce identical results as before","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-28T21:30:55.55377-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/emma","updated_at":"2025-12-28T21:43:50.759453-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-28T21:43:50.759453-08:00"}

- .claude-plugin/*.json
- integrations/beads-mcp/pyproject.toml
- integrations/beads-mcp/src/beads_mcp/__init__.py
- npm-package/package.json
- Hook templates
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md (adds date)

 {"id":"bd-wisp-c3g","title":"mol-refinery-patrol","description":"Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to main one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-10T22:51:49.272262-08:00","updated_at":"2026-01-10T22:51:49.272262-08:00","ephemeral":true}
 {"id":"bd-wisp-cf3","title":"Create release tag","description":"Create annotated git tag.

```bash
git tag -a v0.45.0 -m "Release v0.45.0"
```

Verify: `git tag -l | tail -5`
